Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states autoimmune condition diagnosis: 'autoimmune disease', 'immune system attacks body', 'autoimmune disorder', immunosuppressive treatment, or when there is documented presence of antibodies in the blood circulation directed against the patient's own cells or tissues (a characteristic feature of autoimmune conditions) — NOT for frequent infections, family history of autoimmune conditions, or immune system concerns without autoimmune diagnosis

Name: Dysautonomia
Notes: Only use when dysautonomia is explicitly diagnosed or clearly described (temperature dysregulation, blood pressure instability, heart rate variability). Speculative mentions (seems like autonomic issues) do NOT qualify.

Name: Fevers
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes recurrent unexplained fevers: 'gets fevers frequently for no reason', 'fever every few weeks', 'recurrent fevers', 'unexplained high temperatures' - NOT for single fever episodes, fevers during illness, or normal immune responses to infections.

Name: Infection Risk High
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes unusually high susceptibility to infections: 'gets sick constantly', 'catches every bug', 'infections all the time', 'immune system weak', frequent antibiotic use - NOT for normal childhood illnesses, seasonal infections, or occasional sick episodes without pattern of high susceptibility.

Name: Neoplasm (Includes tumors, cancer, benign tumors)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states tumor or cancer diagnosis: 'tumor', 'cancer', 'benign tumor', 'malignant growth', 'neoplasm', oncology referral, surgical removal of tumor - NOT for concerning lumps under evaluation, family history of cancer, or growths without confirmed tumor diagnosis.

Name: Sepsis
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states 'sepsis' diagnosis: 'blood infection', 'sepsis', 'blood poisoning', 'septic', ICU treatment for sepsis, IV antibiotics for sepsis, or when there is documentation of life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection (which may occur repeatedly) — NOT for severe infections, high fevers, or hospitalization without specific sepsis diagnosis

Name: Perinatal (prenatal, postnatal, birth)
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Perinatal conditions - NOT for coding individual patient perinatal symptoms or diagnoses.

Name: Brain abnormality, prenatal
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states prenatal brain abnormalities found on imaging: 'brain abnormality seen on ultrasound', 'prenatal MRI showed brain problems', 'fetal brain defect', 'brain abnormality detected before birth' - NOT for postnatal brain findings, developmental concerns, or suspected issues without prenatal imaging confirmation.

Name: Choroid plexus cysts
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states choroid plexus cysts found on prenatal imaging: 'choroid plexus cysts on ultrasound', 'CPCs seen prenatally', 'cysts in brain ventricles before birth', or when documentation describes sonographically discrete, small cysts found in the choroid plexus within the lateral cerebral ventricles typically detected between 14 to 24 weeks gestation via transverse plane imaging of the fetal head with bilateral inspection of the choroid plexus — NOT for postnatal brain cysts, other brain abnormalities, cysts found after birth without prenatal detection, or when size of CPCs is the only information provided without confirmation of prenatal detection

Name: Fetal movement, reduced
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly reports decreased fetal movement during pregnancy: 'baby wasn't moving much', 'reduced fetal movement', 'kicks decreased', 'baby was quiet in womb', fetal monitoring for decreased movement - NOT for normal fetal activity variations, late pregnancy reduced space, or subjective concerns without documented decreased movement.

Name: Hydronephrosis
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states hydronephrosis diagnosis: 'kidney swelling', 'enlarged kidney', 'hydronephrosis', 'fluid in kidney', urology referral for hydronephrosis, or specific mention of renal pelvis and calices dilation or distention of the kidney. This includes documented mild pyelectasis (a form of hydronephrosis) showing hypoechoic spherical or elliptical space within the renal pelvis measuring 5-10mm on transverse section imaging. — NOT for UTIs, kidney stones, or general kidney concerns without specific hydronephrosis diagnosis or without explicit documentation of renal pelvis dilation or kidney distention.

Name: Premature
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states baby was born preterm: 'born early', 'premature', 'preterm', 'born before 37 weeks', NICU stay for prematurity - NOT for small babies born at term, early term births (37-39 weeks), or delivery timing concerns without confirmed preterm birth.

Name: Prenatal Renal Abnormalities (Includes renal obstruction)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states prenatal kidney abnormalities: 'kidney problems seen on ultrasound', 'prenatal renal obstruction', 'kidney abnormality before birth', 'fetal kidney defect' - NOT for postnatal kidney issues, UTIs, or kidney concerns without prenatal detection.

Name: Birth weight, abnormal (includes intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR))
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly documents abnormal birth weight: 'IUGR', 'small for gestational age', 'macrosomia', 'birth weight too low/high', 'growth restriction in womb' - NOT for normal weight variations, subjective size concerns, or weight issues developing after birth.

Name: Pregnancy complications (includes threatened abortion, gestational diabetes)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly describes maternal pregnancy complications: 'gestational diabetes', 'threatened abortion', 'pregnancy complications', 'high-risk pregnancy', 'bleeding during pregnancy' - NOT for normal pregnancy discomforts, single episodes, or family history without maternal complications.

Name: Fetal physiology, abnormal (includes jaundice, congenital pneumonia)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states abnormal fetal/neonatal physiology: 'jaundice', 'congenital pneumonia', 'breathing problems at birth', 'yellow skin', phototherapy for jaundice - NOT for normal newborn transitions, temporary issues, or physiological concerns without abnormal diagnosis.

Name: Ultrasound Findings
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states ultrasound showed notable abnormal findings: 'ultrasound showed problems', 'abnormal ultrasound', 'ultrasound found defects', further testing recommended based on ultrasound, soft markers identified at 16-20 weeks gestation that indicate increased risk for fetal aneuploidy or other disorders, or specific abnormal patterns such as sonographic snowstorm pattern of the uterus with complex vesicular intrauterine mass containing grape-like cysts — NOT for routine ultrasounds, normal findings, ultrasounds ordered without confirmed abnormalities, or ultrasound findings that do not represent actual abnormalities requiring follow-up

Name: Amniotic Fluid, Abnormal (includes Oligohydramnios and Polyhydramnios)
Notes: Polyhydraminos HP:0001561'-' excess amniotic fluid, oligohydraminos HP:0001562'-' diminished amniotic fluid.

Name: Skin
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Skin conditions - NOT for coding individual patient skin symptoms or diagnoses. — NOT for coding individual patient skin symptoms or diagnoses, such as red skin eruptions or other specific dermatological manifestations.

Name: Ecchymosis (Bruises Easily)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient bruises much more easily than normal: 'bruises from light touch', 'bruises constantly', 'gets black and blue easily', 'marks from minor bumps' - NOT for normal childhood bruising, occasional bruises, or bruises from obvious trauma.

Name: Excess Skin
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly describes redundant or excess skin: 'extra skin', 'loose skin', 'too much skin', 'redundant skin folds', plastic surgery for excess skin, or specifically excess skin around the neck area that lies in horizontal folds — NOT for normal skin variations, weight-related skin changes, or age-appropriate skin elasticity

Name: Hair Growth, Abnormal (Hypertrichosis, excess or lack of hair)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significantly abnormal hair growth: 'too much hair', 'hypertrichosis', 'very little hair', 'bald patches', 'hair doesn't grow normally' - NOT for normal hair variations, slow hair growth, or age-appropriate hair patterns.

Name: Hair Pattern, Abnormal
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes unusual hair patterns or distribution: 'weird cowlicks', 'hair grows in strange patterns', 'abnormal eyebrow hair', 'hair whorl in wrong place', or other forms of congenital abnormality of hair distribution — NOT for normal cowlicks, family hair patterns, or minor hair direction variations

Name: Nail Defects
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes abnormal nail formation or growth: 'nails are malformed', 'nails don't grow right', 'nail deformity', 'abnormal nail shape', 'nails are brittle/thick', nails that form a tighter curve of convexity when viewed on end (with the digit tip pointing toward the examiner's eye), or decreased width of nail characterized by narrower than normal nail plates — NOT for normal nail variations, occasional nail damage, or nail biting effects

Name: Pigmentation, Abnormal
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes unusual skin pigmentation: 'skin has white patches', 'dark spots', 'pigmentation problems', 'skin color uneven', 'café-au-lait spots' - NOT for normal skin tone variations, temporary color changes, or minor birthmarks.

Name: Sensitive Skin/Dermatitis (Includes eczema, psoriasis, rashes, lumps, bumps)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes chronic skin conditions: 'eczema', 'psoriasis', 'constant rashes', 'sensitive skin breaks out', 'dermatitis', prescription skin medications - NOT for occasional rashes, temporary skin irritation, or single episode skin problems.

Name: Skin Color, Abnormal (Includes flushing, circulation issues, lacy pattern, mottling)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes persistent abnormal skin color patterns: 'skin is mottled', 'lacy pattern on skin', 'flushing constantly', 'circulation problems', 'skin color changes' - NOT for normal color variations, temporary flushing, or situational color changes.

Name: Sweating, Abnormal (Includes absent or reduced sweating, hyperhidrosis)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significantly abnormal sweating: 'sweats excessively', 'hyperhidrosis', 'doesn't sweat at all', 'sweating problems', 'can't regulate temperature through sweating' - NOT for normal sweating variations, situational sweating, or minor sweating differences.

Name: Temperature Regulation, Poor (Includes hot and cold sensitivity)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant temperature regulation problems: 'can't handle heat/cold', 'overheats easily', 'gets too cold quickly', 'temperature regulation problems', 'sensitive to temperature changes' - NOT for normal temperature preferences, seasonal discomfort, or minor temperature sensitivity.

Name: Sleep
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Sleep conditions - NOT for coding individual patient sleep symptoms or diagnoses.

Name: Circadian Abnormalities (sleep wake cycle in 24 hours)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes severely disrupted sleep-wake cycle: 'sleeps all day, awake all night', 'sleep cycle is completely backwards', 'can't get on normal schedule', 'circadian rhythm disorder' - NOT for normal sleep variations, temporary schedule changes, or mild sleep preferences.

Name: Disorientation (Includes when aroused)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient becomes confused or disoriented: 'gets confused when waking up', 'disoriented', 'doesn't know where they are', 'confused after waking' - NOT for normal grogginess, brief confusion during illness, or age-appropriate orientation development.

Name: Drowsiness
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes persistent daytime sleepiness affecting function: 'falls asleep during activities', 'drowsy all day', 'can't stay awake', 'excessive daytime sleepiness', or when there is evidence of a neurological condition involving sudden recurrent uncontrollable compulsion to sleep — NOT for normal tiredness, nap needs, or temporary sleepiness during illness

Name: EEG Abnormal During Sleep
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states sleep EEG was abnormal: 'sleep EEG showed problems', 'abnormal brain waves during sleep', 'sleep study EEG abnormal' - NOT for sleep studies without EEG, normal sleep EEG, or EEG ordered without confirmed abnormal results.

Name: Initiation and Maintenance Difficulties of Sleep (Includes dyssomnia, restlessness, need for physical proximity to caregiver)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes persistent sleep initiation or maintenance problems: 'can't fall asleep', 'wakes up constantly', 'needs parent to sleep', 'dyssomnia', 'restless sleep every night' - NOT for occasional sleep difficulties, temporary sleep disruptions, or age-appropriate sleep needs.

Name: Narcolepsy/Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states narcolepsy diagnosis or excessive daytime sleepiness: 'narcolepsy', 'falls asleep suddenly', 'excessive daytime sleepiness', narcolepsy medication - NOT for normal tiredness, occasional sleepiness, or drowsiness during illness.

Name: Restless Legs
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes restless leg syndrome symptoms: 'legs feel restless', 'RLS', 'can't keep legs still', 'uncomfortable leg sensations', 'needs to move legs constantly', occurs after going to bed, often leading to insomnia — NOT for normal fidgeting, occasional leg movement, or general restlessness

Name: Sleep Apnea (Includes snoring)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes sleep apnea or significant snoring: 'stops breathing during sleep', 'sleep apnea', 'loud snoring', 'CPAP machine', 'BiPAP', sleep study showing apnea - NOT for occasional snoring, mouth breathing, or normal sleep sounds.

Name: Sleep Duration, Abnormal
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significantly abnormal sleep duration: 'sleeps only 3 hours', 'sleeps 16 hours a day', 'sleep time is way off for age', 'unusual sleep duration', or reports abnormalities in quality and characteristics of sleep — NOT for normal sleep variations, short naps, or temporary sleep changes during illness

Name: Parasomnia (includes sleepwalking)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes parasomnia behaviors: 'sleepwalking', 'night behaviors', 'does things while sleeping', 'parasomnia', 'acts out dreams' - NOT for restless sleep, talking in sleep, or seizures during sleep.

Name: SUDEP (seizure in sleep causing death)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly discusses SUDEP risk or occurrence: 'SUDEP risk', 'sudden death from seizures', 'seizure-related death risk', SUDEP counseling provided - NOT for general seizure concerns, sleep seizures without SUDEP discussion, or seizure safety without SUDEP risk.

Name: Teeth Grinding (includes when awake bruxism)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes frequent teeth grinding: 'grinds teeth', 'bruxism', 'clenches jaw', 'teeth grinding at night', dental guard needed - NOT for occasional jaw clenching, stress-related grinding, or single episodes of grinding.

Name: Terrors, night terrors (unable to describe)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes night terrors: 'night terrors', 'screams in sleep but can't remember', 'terror episodes at night', 'wakes up scared but can't explain' - NOT for nightmares with recall, bad dreams, or fears that can be described.

Name: Visual
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Visual conditions - NOT for coding individual patient visual symptoms or diagnoses. Loss of visual acuity implies vision was better at a certain time point in life, distinct from reduced visual acuity. — NOT for coding individual patient visual symptoms or diagnoses, and NOT to be used when there is no prior better vision implied (in which case 'reduced visual acuity' or a subclass would be more appropriate).

Name: Alacrima (absent tears)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly describes absent tear production: 'doesn't make tears', 'eyes don't water', 'alacrima', 'can't cry tears', 'dry eyes from no tears' - NOT for occasional dry eyes, reduced crying, or temporary tear reduction.

Name: Amblyopia (Includes lazy eye)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states lazy eye diagnosis: 'lazy eye', 'amblyopia', 'one eye doesn't work as well', eye patch treatment, vision therapy for lazy eye - NOT for vision differences, eye alignment issues, or visual concerns without amblyopia diagnosis.

Name: Blindness
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states patient is blind or has severe vision loss: 'blind', 'can't see', 'no vision', 'legally blind', braille instruction, guide dog training, visual acuity less than 3/60 on the 6m scale, less than 20/400 on the 20ft scale, less than 0.05 on the decimal scale, or visual field of no greater than 10 degrees in radius around central fixation. Also include when blindness with onset specifically at birth is documented. — NOT for poor vision, vision impairment, or reduced vision without blindness diagnosis, or when visual acuity or visual field measurements exceed the thresholds for clinical blindness.

Name: Cataracts
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states cataract diagnosis: 'cataracts', 'cloudy lens', 'cataract surgery', 'lens opacity', or specifically mentions opacity/clouding that develops in the crystalline lens of the eye or its capsule. This includes presenile cataracts occurring in early adulthood or posterior subcapsular cataracts with iridescent color. — NOT for general vision problems, eye cloudiness without cataract diagnosis, vision changes without specific cataract finding, or any visual impairment not explicitly associated with lens opacity.

Name: Cortical Visual Impairment (Includes suspected but not proven)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states cortical visual impairment: 'CVI', 'cortical visual impairment', 'brain doesn't process vision', 'eyes work but brain doesn't see' - NOT for general vision problems, refractive errors, or developmental vision concerns without CVI diagnosis.

Name: Eye Movement, Abnormal (includes dystonic)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes abnormal eye movements: 'eyes move strangely', 'dystonic eye movements', 'eyes jerk', 'abnormal eye tracking', 'eyes don't move together' - NOT for normal eye movement variations, occasional eye wandering, or tracking issues during development.

Name: Nystagmus (Includes Nystagmus'-'like, opsoclonus'-'like eye movement issue, aberrant gaze saccades)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician describes involuntary eye movements: 'nystagmus', 'eyes shake', 'dancing eyes', 'opsoclonus', 'eyes move back and forth on their own' - NOT for voluntary eye movements, normal tracking, or occasional eye fluttering.

Name: Oculogyric Crisis
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly describes oculogyric crisis episodes: 'eyes roll up and get stuck', 'oculogyric crisis', 'eyes deviate upward involuntarily', emergency treatment for eye crisis, blepharospasm, periorbital twitches, protracted fixed staring episodes, and maximal upward deviation of the eyes in a sustained fashion. Oculogyric crisis can be triggered by a number of factors including neuroleptic medications. — NOT for normal eye rolling, voluntary eye movements, or other eye movement abnormalities.

Name: Eye, Anatomical Abnormalities (includes epicanthic folds, downslanting palpebral fissures, asymetrical ptosis, puffy eyelids, long eyelashes)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician describes structural eye abnormalities: 'droopy eyelids', 'ptosis', 'epicanthic folds', 'eye shape abnormal', 'puffy eyelids', surgery for eyelid problems - NOT for normal eye variations, family eye features, or minor asymmetry without structural abnormalities.

Name: Light Sensitivity (photosensitive)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant light sensitivity: 'can't tolerate bright lights', 'photosensitive', 'squints in normal light', 'needs sunglasses indoors', 'light hurts eyes' - NOT for normal light preferences, temporary light sensitivity, or occasional squinting in bright sun.

Name: Optic Nerve Abnormality (includes atrophy, hypoplasia)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states optic nerve abnormalities: 'optic nerve atrophy', 'optic nerve hypoplasia', 'optic nerve doesn't work right', 'pale optic nerve', ophthalmology treatment for optic nerve - NOT for general vision problems, eye issues, or visual concerns without specific optic nerve abnormality.

Name: Strabismus (includes abnormal eye movements, tracking difficulties, exotropia)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly states eye alignment problems: 'strabismus', 'crossed eyes', 'wall eyes', 'exotropia', 'eyes don't line up', eye surgery for alignment - NOT for occasional eye wandering, tracking difficulties during development, or normal eye coordination variations.

Name: Visual Impairment (includes peripheral vision loss, nearsightedness, farsightedness, needs glasses)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant vision problems requiring correction: 'needs glasses', 'nearsighted', 'farsighted', 'can't see well', 'vision impairment', vision services needed - NOT for normal vision variations, occasional squinting, or vision concerns without documented impairment.

Name: Visual'-'Spatial Issues (Includes depth perception)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes visual-spatial processing problems: 'can't judge distances', 'depth perception problems', 'bumps into things', 'visual-spatial issues', 'trouble with spatial relationships' - NOT for occasional clumsiness, normal exploration, or minor coordination issues without visual-spatial processing concerns.

Name: INDIVIDUAL IMPACTS
Notes: ONLY use as a category header for organizing Individual Impact concepts - NOT for coding individual patient impacts or symptoms.

Name: Distal Impacts
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Distal Impact conditions - NOT for coding individual patient distal impacts or symptoms.

Name: Education Impact
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly describes condition affecting educational progress: 'condition affects school', 'education is impacted', 'school performance suffers', 'learning is affected by condition' - NOT for normal academic challenges, age-appropriate learning variations, or educational concerns unrelated to medical condition.

Name: Absenteeism
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes frequent school absences due to medical condition: 'misses school often because of condition', 'frequent absences', 'out of school a lot', 'condition causes school absences' - NOT for occasional sick days, normal absences, or absences unrelated to the medical condition.

Name: Academic Support Needed, IEP, Individualized Education Plan
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states special education services: 'has an IEP', '504 plan', 'special education', 'always has a para', 'individualized education plan' - NOT for general tutoring, occasional help, or academic support without formal special education documentation.

Name: Curriculum Adaptations
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes modified curriculum for the patient: 'curriculum is adapted', 'modified assignments', 'different curriculum', 'school work is changed for condition' - NOT for normal accommodations, extra time, or teaching style adjustments without curriculum modification.

Name: Diet '-' specialized implementation necessary
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes medically necessary special diet: 'special diet required', 'restricted diet for condition', 'medical diet', 'can only eat certain foods', nutritionist involvement, or cases where symptoms are improved or made more bearable by following a ketogenic diet — NOT for food preferences, picky eating, or dietary choices without medical necessity

Name: Educational Setting Restricted
Notes: Home'-'based setting Necessary, Home school

Name: Nursing, in'-'school
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states school nursing services: 'has a nurse at school', 'school nurse needed', 'in-school nursing', 'medical care at school' - NOT for occasional nurse visits, medication reminders, or general health monitoring without dedicated nursing services.

Name: Shortened school day
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver states reduced school hours due to condition: 'mornings only', 'half days', 'goes to school at 10 AM', 'shortened day', 'early dismissal needed' - NOT for normal early dismissal, after-school activities, or schedule preferences without medical necessity.

Name: Literacy and/or writing, severely delayed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes severe reading/writing delays compared to peers: 'can't read at all', 'writing is severely behind', 'literacy is way below grade level', 'severely delayed reading/writing' - NOT for mild delays, learning differences, or age-appropriate literacy development variations.

Name: Social and Family Impact
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes condition significantly affecting social/family functioning: 'condition impacts family life', 'affects social situations', 'family/social functioning suffers' - NOT for normal family challenges, minor social adjustments, or typical developmental social changes.

Name: Behaviors are challenging in social situations, such as yelling, stubbornness, demanding, or withdrawn
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes behaviors that significantly disrupt social settings: 'yells in public', 'stubborn in social situations', 'withdrawn from groups', 'demanding behavior embarrasses family' - NOT for occasional social challenges, age-appropriate assertiveness, or mild social preferences.

Name: Communication, difficult to be understood outside the family
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver states patient's communication is clear to family but unclear to others: 'only family understands speech', 'teachers can't understand', 'strangers don't know what they're saying', 'communication only works with family' - NOT for mild articulation issues, age-appropriate speech development, or occasional communication difficulties.

Name: Engaged socially (Capable of social engagement, establishing intentional relationships)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes clear social engagement and relationships: 'makes friends', 'socially engaged', 'establishes relationships', 'connects with others', 'social reciprocity present' - NOT for minimal social interaction, parallel play, or social behaviors without genuine engagement/relationships.

Name: Extracurricular activities require adaptations
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes activities needing modification for patient participation: 'sports require adaptations', 'activities must be modified', 'needs accommodations for clubs', 'adaptations needed for participation', including difficulty performing daily activities like eating, bathing, dressing, grooming, work, and homemaking, which may require adaptations or modifications for participation — NOT for choosing easier activities, natural modifications, or activities suited to abilities without formal adaptations

Name: Impulsive or Inappropriate social behavior (may greet people with grabbing, overly friendly)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes socially inappropriate impulsive behaviors: 'grabs strangers', 'hugs everyone', 'touches people's faces', 'overly friendly with strangers', 'inappropriate social impulses' - NOT for normal friendliness, age-appropriate social behavior, or mild social boundary issues.

Name: Laughter, Inappropriate (not typical social context)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes laughing at inappropriate times: 'laughs during serious conversations', 'laughs at others' pain', 'inappropriate laughter', 'laughs for no reason in wrong situations' - NOT for nervous laughter, age-appropriate humor, or occasional inappropriate timing.

Name: Law enforcement encounters - includes security guards and police (individual impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes actual encounters with law enforcement due to patient behaviors: 'police called', 'security involved', 'law enforcement encounters', 'arrested', 'police intervention needed' - NOT for general safety concerns, hypothetical scenarios, or family fears without actual encounters.

Name: Navigating new environments, needs assistance
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient requires help in new environments: 'needs help in new places', 'can't navigate unfamiliar areas', 'gets lost easily', 'requires assistance in new environments' - NOT for normal caution in new places, age-appropriate supervision needs, or mild orientation challenges.

Name: Safety (cross street, elopement, burn risk), Judgement Impaired, Danger Awareness Decreased
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant safety concerns due to impaired judgment: 'runs into traffic', 'elopes/wanders', 'no danger awareness', 'burns self', 'doesn't understand safety' - NOT for normal risk-taking, age-appropriate safety learning, or mild impulsivity without significant danger.

Name: Sibling relationships, typical
Notes: Only use when caregiver explicitly describes TYPICAL/NORMAL sibling interactions or relationships. The mere mention of siblings does not qualify.

Name: Social isolation (includes isolation because of treatments, inability to communicate, inability to move)
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [306] Activities of Daily Living (ADL): Requires 100% Support  . | DO NOT confuse with [107] Reflux (Includes GERD).

Name: Sports or Play with others (difficult or impossible)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient cannot participate in sports/play with others: 'can't play with other kids', 'sports are impossible', 'too difficult to play with others', 'needs help to play' - NOT for sports preferences, choosing individual activities, or age-appropriate play variations.

Name: Transitioning issues (difficulty adapting to change, routine needed, rigid behavior)
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [72] Speech, Absent (Non-speaking).

Name: Work Impact
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes condition affecting current or future work ability: 'condition will affect work', 'can't work because of condition', 'work is impacted', 'employment affected' - NOT for normal career concerns, educational planning, or work preferences without condition-related impact.

Name: Work, Difficult or Impossible
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes work participation is severely limited: 'can't work', 'work is impossible', 'unable to hold a job', 'work is too difficult', 'employment not possible' - NOT for career challenges, job preferences, or normal work-related concerns without severe functional limitations.

Name: Work, Reduced Schedule
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient requires reduced work schedule due to condition: 'works part-time because of condition', 'reduced hours needed', 'can't work full-time', 'schedule reduced for medical reasons' - NOT for normal part-time preferences, educational scheduling, or work choices unrelated to medical condition.

Name: Proximal Impacts
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Proximal Impact conditions - NOT for coding individual patient proximal impacts or symptoms.

Name: Health Impact (individual impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes overall health burden on patient: 'condition affects overall health', 'health is impacted', 'illness burden', 'fatigue from condition', 'health suffers' - NOT for specific symptoms already coded elsewhere, normal tiredness, or temporary health issues.

Name: Appointments, Frequent (individual impact)
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [107] Reflux (Includes GERD).

Name: Hospitalizations, Frequent (individual impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes multiple hospitalizations or frequent hospital stays: 'hospitalized several times', 'frequent hospitalizations', 'in hospital a lot', 'multiple admissions', 'recurring hospital stays' - NOT for single hospitalizations, planned procedures, or emergency visits without pattern of frequent admissions.

Name: ICU
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states patient required intensive care: 'was in ICU', 'intensive care unit', 'critical care', 'life support', 'ICU stay' - NOT for regular hospital stays, monitoring, or emergency department visits without ICU-level care.

Name: NICU
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states patient required neonatal intensive care: 'was in NICU', 'neonatal intensive care', 'special care nursery', 'NICU baby' - NOT for normal newborn nursery, brief observation, or routine postnatal care without intensive neonatal care.

Name: Overall Health Impaired
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient's overall health as significantly compromised: 'overall health is poor', 'health is impaired', 'generally unhealthy', 'health problems affect everything', or when there is diminished physical functioning showing reduction in ability to perform activities of daily living compared to previous abilities due to functional deficits from illness, which may be measured using tools like the 36-item Short Form (SF-36) health survey questionnaire — NOT for specific conditions already coded, temporary illness, normal health variations, or when diminished functioning is limited to a specific activity without affecting overall health status

Name: Cancer Risk Increased
Notes: Gene mutation confers a higher lifetime risk of developing cancer

Name: Falls
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [200] Hypotonia. If the rationale describes low muscle tone, floppy posture, or inability to hold self up, use Hypotonia instead. Only use Falls when actual falling events are described.

Name: Mortality Risk Increased
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver or clinician explicitly discusses elevated death risk: 'risk of death', 'life-threatening condition', 'mortality risk', 'could be fatal', 'life expectancy affected' - NOT for general health concerns, serious conditions without mortality discussion, or family fears without clinical mortality risk assessment.

Name: Pain
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes pain as frequent or significant issue: 'has pain often', 'chronic pain', 'pain is a problem', 'hurts frequently', pain medication needed, or when there is an unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual or potential tissue damage specifically related to multiple subcutaneous lipoma — NOT for occasional discomfort, temporary pain during illness, or minor aches without significant pain pattern

Name: Resuscitation Needed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states resuscitation was required: 'needed to be resuscitated', 'CPR performed', 'stopped breathing and needed help', 'resuscitation required', emergency resuscitation - NOT for breathing support, medical emergencies without resuscitation, or respiratory assistance without actual resuscitation.

Name: Severely Ill
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient as severely ill: 'severely ill', 'very sick', 'critically ill', 'life-threatening illness', 'gravely ill' - NOT for normal illness, chronic conditions without severity, or temporary sickness without severe illness description.

Name: Suffering
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly states patient is suffering: 'suffering', 'in agony', 'enduring pain', 'tremendous suffering', 'quality of life poor due to suffering' - NOT for discomfort, normal illness effects, or challenges without explicit suffering description.

Name: Perinatal
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes perinatal events as focus of current impact: 'birth complications still affecting', 'perinatal issues continue to impact', 'prenatal problems affecting now' - NOT for resolved perinatal events, past birth history without current impact, or perinatal factors not currently affecting patient.

Name: Surgical/Medical Interventions, Necessary, Frequent
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes repeated procedures or frequent medical interventions: 'frequent surgeries', 'procedures all the time', 'repeated medical interventions', 'constant procedures needed' - NOT for single procedures, routine follow-ups, or occasional medical interventions without frequency pattern.

Name: Self Care and Daily Living Impact
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant impacts on patient's self-care and daily living activities: 'can't dress himself', 'needs help with all daily activities', 'daily living severely affected', 'self-care is impossible', including substantial difficulties with eating, bathing, grooming, work, homemaking, or leisure activities. — NOT for minor assistance needs, age-appropriate dependence, or temporary help without significant daily living impact.

Name: Activities of Daily Living (ADL): Requires 100% Support
Notes: lacks independence, includes all levels of assistance

Name: Assistance with Activities of Daily Living (ADL): Requires Partial Support
Notes: Capable of Self'-'Care; need assistance with independent living

Name: Assisted Living Facility (ALF)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver states patient resides in assisted living facility: 'lives in assisted living', 'ALF resident', 'assisted living facility', 'residential care facility' - NOT for nursing homes, group homes, or independent living with services without formal assisted living facility placement.

Name: Bathing assistance needed (includes all hygiene)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient needs help with bathing or hygiene: 'needs help bathing', 'can't wash self', 'hygiene assistance required', 'help with brushing teeth' - NOT for normal supervision, reminders for hygiene, or age-appropriate assistance without ongoing need.

Name: Dressing assistance needed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient needs help with dressing: 'needs help getting dressed', 'can't put on clothes', 'dressing assistance required', 'help with buttons/zippers' - NOT for choosing clothes, normal supervision, or age-appropriate dressing development.

Name: Eating and drinking assistance needed
Notes: Feeding

Name: Following complex directions
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient cannot follow multi-step directions: 'can't follow complex directions', 'struggles with multi-step instructions', 'needs simple directions only', 'complex directions are too hard' - NOT for age-appropriate direction-following, occasional confusion, or need for clear instructions without complexity issues.

Name: Household tasks assistance needed (includes preparing food)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient needs help with household tasks: 'can't do chores', 'needs help with meal prep', 'household tasks too difficult', 'assistance with cooking needed' - NOT for age-appropriate chore expectations, normal supervision, or tasks beyond patient's developmental level.

Name: Mobility assistance needed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient needs help moving around: 'needs help walking', 'mobility assistance required', 'wheelchair needed', 'help getting around', 'mobility equipment needed' - NOT for normal supervision, occasional assistance, or mobility preferences without assistance requirement.

Name: Regression in Activities of Daily Living (ADL)
Notes: Only use when caregiver explicitly describes LOSS of previously acquired ADL skills (e.g., used to dress independently, now cannot). General difficulty with ADLs is [307] Assistance with ADL. Regression in other domains should use the specific regression code.

Name: Toileting assistance needed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes patient needs help with toileting: 'needs help with bathroom', 'toileting assistance required', 'uses pull-ups', 'help with bowel/bladder', requires help transferring to the toilet, needs assistance cleaning themselves, or uses bedpan or commode — NOT for normal potty training, occasional accidents, or age-appropriate toilet learning without ongoing assistance need

Name: CAREGIVER IMPACTS
Notes: ONLY use as a category header for organizing Caregiver Impact concepts - NOT for coding individual caregiver impacts or symptoms.

Name: Caregiver and Family Burden
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Caregiver and Family Burden conditions - NOT for coding individual caregiver burden impacts or symptoms.

Name: Caregiver Effort Requirement
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes high workload due to patient care: 'takes a lot of effort', 'caregiving is demanding', 'requires significant care', 'high care needs', 'exhausting to care for' - NOT for normal parenting efforts, routine care, or typical child supervision without excessive effort description.

Name: Advocacy Role, advocacy for patient provided by caregiver
Notes: Pritoriy of patient needs also fits here

Name: Appointments, frequent (caregiver impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes frequent appointment management burden: 'always at appointments', 'appointments consume my time', 'frequent appointment coordination', 'appointment burden is high' - NOT for routine medical follow-ups, occasional appointments, or normal medical management without frequency burden.

Name: Diagnostic odyssey
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes prolonged, complex path to diagnosis: 'diagnostic odyssey', 'long road to diagnosis', 'years to get diagnosis', 'complex diagnostic journey', 'many doctors before diagnosis' - NOT for normal diagnostic process, single specialist referrals, or straightforward diagnostic workup.

Name: Hospitalizations, frequent (caregiver impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes repeated hospitalizations causing caregiver stress/burden: 'hospitalized frequently', 'always in the hospital', 'multiple hospital stays', 'hospital visits are overwhelming' - NOT for single hospitalizations, planned admissions, or hospital visits without caregiver burden description.

Name: Medical Care'-' medical interventions must be provided by caregiver and family members(medications, bowl wash out, chest physio, etc.)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes providing complex medical care at home: 'give medications at home', 'do chest physio', 'perform medical procedures', 'provide medical care', 'home medical interventions' - NOT for simple medication administration, basic care tasks, or medical care provided by professionals without family involvement.

Name: Time needed for care (e.g. feeding, morning & bedtime routines, organization of medical care)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant time consumption for patient care: 'care takes hours', 'routines consume my day', 'medical care organization takes all my time', 'care is very time-consuming' - NOT for normal parenting time, routine care schedules, or typical daily activities without excessive time burden.

Name: Daily Living Impacts
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes significant impacts on daily living: 'daily life is affected', 'condition impacts our daily routine', 'daily living is disrupted', 'affects how we live daily' - includes impacts on activities of daily living such as eating, bathing, dressing, grooming, work, homemaking, and leisure — NOT for minor adjustments, normal parenting adaptations, or routine accommodations without significant daily living impact

Name: Formal Support System Needed
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes need for formal support services: 'need respite services', 'formal supports required', 'agency help needed', 'professional support services' - NOT for informal family help, normal community resources, or occasional assistance without formal support system needs.

Name: Family members
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [307] Assistance with Activities of Daily Living (ADL): Requires Partial Support. | This code is for when the caregiver describes the IMPACT on family members (burden, stress, changes in their lives). Do NOT use just because family members are mentioned as providing help or existing.

Name: Government Assistance
Notes: ONLY use when public assistance programs are explicitly used or sought: 'applying for government assistance', 'receiving public aid', 'disability benefits', 'welfare assistance', 'government support programs' - NOT for insurance coverage, private assistance, or theoretical discussion of benefits without actual use/seeking.

Name: Nursing 24 hr
Notes: ONLY use when 24-hour nursing is in place or needed: '24-hour nursing', 'round-the-clock nursing', '24/7 nursing care', 'continuous nursing', 'full-time nursing' - NOT for part-time nursing, occasional nursing visits, or nursing assistance without 24-hour requirement.

Name: Other Caregivers
Notes: ONLY use when non-parent caregivers are involved: 'grandparents help with care', 'babysitter provides care', 'other family caregivers', 'non-parent caregiver', 'extended family helps' - NOT for normal babysitting, occasional help, or parent-only care without other caregivers.

Name: Respite
Notes: ONLY use when respite services are used or needed: 'respite care', 'need respite services', 'respite provider', 'formal respite', 'respite program' - NOT for normal babysitting, family help, or temporary care without formal respite services.

Name: Household tasks (difficult to complete), includes caring for other children
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver describes difficulty completing household tasks due to patient care: 'can't keep up with housework', 'household tasks are overwhelming', 'hard to care for other children', 'home management is difficult' - NOT for normal household management, temporary busy periods, or household tasks without care-related difficulty.

Name: Emotional Impact
Notes: ONLY use when explicitly stated emotional impact for patient/caregiver: 'emotional impact', 'emotionally affected', 'emotional consequences', 'affects emotions', 'emotional toll', increased tendency to express emotions compared to previous norm, or reduction in tendency to express emotions noticed as a behavior change by family/peers — NOT for ruled-out situations, hypothetical scenarios, or screening mentions without actual emotional impact

Name: Anxiety and stress
Notes: ONLY use when clinician/caregiver reports persistent anxiety: 'anxiety disorder', 'panic attacks', 'social anxiety', 'separation anxiety', 'persistent stress', 'chronic anxiety', or when there are intense feelings of nervousness, tension, panic in response to interpersonal stresses, feelings of being fearful, apprehensive, or threatened by uncertainty, fears of falling apart or losing control, future-oriented worry accompanied by negative affect and autonomic arousal, or excessive fear of embarrassment, humiliation, or rejection when exposed to possible negative evaluation by others during public performance or social interactions — NOT for normal worry, temporary stress, or situational anxiety without persistence

Name: Denial
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver initially denied/struggled to accept diagnosis: 'denied diagnosis', 'struggled to accept', 'refused to believe', 'denial phase', 'didn't accept condition' - NOT for normal questioning, seeking second opinions, or acceptance without denial phase.

Name: Depression and sadness
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [314] Mobility assistance needed.

Name: Embarrassment (managing behavior of child in public)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver feels embarrassed managing behaviors in public: 'embarrassed in public', 'ashamed of behaviors', 'humiliated in public', 'embarrassing public behaviors', 'public embarrassment' - NOT for normal parenting challenges, mild discomfort, or private embarrassment without public component.

Name: Fear
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver expresses fear related to condition: 'afraid for child', 'fear about future', 'scared of condition', 'fearful about health', 'fear for safety', which may include observable fear-induced behaviors and avoidance of fear-inducing situations at abnormal levels — NOT for normal parenting concerns, caution, or worry without specific fear, or when fear responses are at normal levels

Name: Frustration (caregiver impact)
Notes: Use when caregiver expresses frustration related to their caregiving experience. This INCLUDES frustration about: the child's condition, systemic barriers to access/resources, limitations of services or the world to support the child, dealing with insurance/bureaucracy, lack of understanding from others. Broader than just interpersonal frustration.

Name: Grief Reaction
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [26] Repetitive behaviors (includes OCD, hand-wringing, compulsions, rituals, loud repetitive vocalizations).

Name: Guilt
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver expresses guilt related to condition: 'feel guilty', 'blame myself', 'guilt about condition', 'guilty I caused this', 'self-blame' where the guilt is excessive, intense, out of proportion, based on incorrect judgment of responsibility, and is pervasive and enduring rather than fleeting or temporary — NOT for normal responsibility, minor regrets, or concern without guilt, or when feelings of guilt are proportionate, fleeting, or temporary

Name: Impact on intimate relationship, marital strain, divorce
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [281] Safety (cross street, elopement, burn risk), Judgement Impaired, Danger Awareness Decreased. | Only use when caregiver explicitly describes strain on intimate/marital relationship or divorce. General family stress or caregiving burden is NOT this code.

Name: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver has PTSD or trauma responses: 'PTSD', 'post-traumatic stress', 'trauma symptoms', 'flashbacks', 'hypervigilance from caregiving trauma' - NOT for normal stress, anxiety, or worry without PTSD diagnosis or trauma responses.

Name: Resentment
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver expresses resentment about caregiving burden: 'resentful about caregiving', 'bitter about situation', 'resentment toward condition', 'angry about care burden' - NOT for temporary frustration, normal emotions, or disappointment without explicit resentment.

Name: Substance abuse, includes alcoholism
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver reports problematic substance use: 'drinking too much', 'substance abuse', 'alcoholism', 'drug problem', 'addiction issues', including addiction to inhaled nitrous oxide gas (N2O) — NOT for occasional drinking, social use, or substances without abuse/dependence pattern

Name: Vigilance and flexibility needed to care for child
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver must constantly monitor/adapt to child's needs: 'constant vigilance required', '24/7 monitoring', 'hypervigilant', 'constant flexibility needed', 'always watching' - NOT for normal parenting supervision, routine monitoring, or occasional vigilance."icular bone drop,  Cal...)

Name: Financial Impact
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [365] Socialization and leisure activities reduced or modified. | Only use when caregiver explicitly describes financial costs, insurance issues, work reduction due to costs, or economic hardship related to the condition. General mention of services received does NOT imply financial impact.

Name: Destruction of possessions by child
Notes: ONLY use when child damages property causing financial/household impact: 'child destroys things', 'breaks possessions', 'property damage', 'destructive behavior costs money', 'damages belongings' - NOT for normal wear and tear, accidents, or minor breakage without significant impact.

Name: Financial burden
Notes: ONLY use when costs strain finances: 'financial burden', 'can't afford costs', 'financially struggling', 'costs overwhelming', 'economic hardship' - NOT for manageable expenses, covered costs, or minor financial impact without burden.

Name: Home or vehicle modifications
Notes: includes moving or new car

Name: Work, reduced capacity
Notes: Only use when caregiver explicitly describes REDUCED WORK capacity: fewer hours, had to quit job, can't work full-time, turned down promotions, reduced responsibilities. Do NOT use for financial strain without actual work reduction — that belongs in [348] Financial Impact. Do NOT use for general stress about balancing work and caregiving without actual capacity reduction.

Name: Health Impact (caregiver impact)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver health is affected by caregiving: 'caregiving affects my health', 'health problems from stress', 'caregiver health declining', 'sick from caregiving' - NOT for normal tiredness, minor aches, or health issues unrelated to caregiving.

Name: Fatigue, exhaustion
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver reports fatigue/exhaustion characterized by a lack of energy and motivation, with expressions such as: 'exhausted', 'so tired', 'fatigue from caregiving', 'worn out', 'constantly tired' - may be considered clinically significant when it persists for six months or longer. — NOT for normal tiredness, temporary fatigue, or situations that might cause fatigue without explicit reports.

Name: Injuries (risk of falling and increased injuries due to assisting younger children exploratory behaviors, impulse control)
Notes: Only use when caregiver describes ACTUAL injuries that have occurred, not general risk of falling or hypothetical injury scenarios. For fall risk without actual injury, use [297] Falls.

Name: Medical and therapy appointments for parents increased due to issues caused by child's disorder (injuries, psychiatric)
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver increases own appointments due to caregiving stress/injuries: 'more doctor visits for stress', 'therapy for caregiver', 'increased medical appointments', 'psychiatric care from caregiving' - NOT for routine care, child's appointments, or unrelated medical visits.

Name: Sleep, disrupted
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver explicitly describes disrupted sleep for the patient OR the caregiver: "can't sleep", "sleep problems", "up all night", "sleep interrupted", "wakes up multiple times", or other clear descriptions of abnormal sleep patterns. In the rationale, specify WHETHER this applies to the patient or the caregiver. NOT for ruled-out sleep issues, hypothetical scenarios, or screening mentions without actual sleep disruption. NOTE: P8 team is considering splitting this into separate patient/caregiver codes.

Name: Reproductive/Family Planning
Notes: ONLY use when caregiving influences decisions about having more children: 'don't want more kids', 'can't handle another child', 'family planning affected', 'decided against more children', 'caregiving limits family size' - NOT for normal family planning, unrelated reproductive decisions, or theoretical discussions.

Name: Social Impacts
Notes: HIGH REJECTION RATE. This is a CATEGORY header. Do NOT use directly — use specific sub-codes instead (e.g., [365] Socialization reduced, [360] Stigma, [361] Social isolation).

Name: Independence, mobility, and agency decreased due to child's need for daily care
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver's independence is reduced due to care needs: 'can't leave child', 'tied to caregiving', 'independence lost', 'mobility limited by care', 'no freedom' - NOT for normal parenting responsibilities, temporary limitations, or choices without actual independence reduction.

Name: Law enforcement encounters - includes security guards and police (caregiver impact)
Notes: (due to behaviors of patient)

Name: Privacy, Loss of
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver privacy is reduced due to care tasks: 'no privacy', 'lost privacy', 'can't have private time', 'privacy invaded', 'personal space lost' - NOT for normal family life, temporary privacy loss, or minor interruptions without significant privacy reduction.

Name: Siblings and family impacted
Notes: ONLY use when siblings/family roles are significantly affected: 'siblings affected', 'family roles changed', 'family activities modified', 'siblings' lives disrupted', 'family dynamics altered' - NOT for minor family adjustments, temporary changes, or normal family adaptations without significant impact.

Name: Social Isolation, Loneliness
Notes: ONLY use when explicitly stated social isolation/loneliness for patient/caregiver: 'socially isolated', 'feel lonely', 'cut off from friends', 'social isolation', 'lonely from caregiving', or when there is a documented reduction in the tendency to seek new interpersonal relationships or maintain existing relationships — NOT for ruled-out situations, temporary isolation, or minor social limitations without actual isolation/loneliness

Name: Socialization and leisure activities reduced or modified
Notes: ONLY use when explicitly stated socialization/leisure activities are reduced/modified for patient/caregiver: 'can't socialize', 'leisure activities limited', 'social activities reduced', 'activities modified', 'socialization decreased', abnormal limitation to a few interests and activities, or reduction in the sharing of information or emotions compared to the person's previous norms — NOT for ruled-out situations, temporary changes, or minor modifications without significant reduction

Name: Stigma
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver reports stigma from others: 'people judge us', 'stigma from others', 'discriminated against', 'people stare and judge', 'stigmatized by community' - NOT for normal social awkwardness, curiosity from others, or self-consciousness without actual stigma from others.

Name: Diagnostic Journey
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Diagnostic Journey conditions - NOT for coding individual diagnostic journey experiences or symptoms.

Name: Age of Diagnosis
Notes: ONLY use when age of diagnosis is mentioned: 'diagnosed at age 3', 'age 5 diagnosis', 'diagnosed when 2 years old', 'age at diagnosis' - NOT for current age, developmental ages, or ages without diagnosis context.

Name: Challenges
Notes: ONLY use when difficulty in getting diagnosis or diagnostic process stress: 'hard to get diagnosis', 'diagnostic challenges', 'difficult diagnosis process', 'challenges getting diagnosed', 'diagnostic uncertainty' - NOT for post-diagnosis challenges, treatment difficulties, or challenges unrelated to diagnostic process.

Name: Genetic testing
Notes: ONLY use when genetic testing is mentioned: 'genetic testing', 'microarray testing', 'chromosomal analysis', 'genetic workup', 'DNA testing' - NOT for family history discussions, hereditary concerns, or genetic counseling without actual testing.

Name: Initial Diagnosis (misdiagnosis)
Notes: ONLY use when initial incorrect diagnosis is described: 'initially diagnosed with', 'first diagnosis was wrong', 'misdiagnosed initially', 'original diagnosis incorrect' - NOT for diagnostic uncertainty, rule-outs, or correct initial diagnoses.

Name: Initial Symptoms
Notes: ONLY use when first signs/symptoms at onset are listed: 'first symptoms were', 'initial signs', 'early symptoms', 'symptoms at onset', 'first noticed' - NOT for current symptoms, later symptoms, or general symptom discussions without initial context.

Name: Medical Provider
Notes: ONLY use when specific provider involvement is mentioned: 'pediatrician involved', 'specialist consulted', 'neurologist referred', 'specific doctor', 'medical provider' - NOT for general medical care, appointments, or provider types without specific involvement.

Name: Missed Milestones
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver mentions missed/late milestones: 'missed milestones', 'delayed development', 'late to walk', 'not talking on time', 'developmental delays' in various domains (motor skills, speech and language, cognitive skills, and social and emotional skills) specifically for children younger than five years of age — NOT for normal variation, early milestones, developmental discussions without missed/late context, or for children five years of age or older

Name: MODIFYING FACTORS
Notes: ONLY use as a category header for organizing Modifying Factor concepts - NOT for coding individual modifying factors or circumstances.

Name: Access Modifier
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Access Modifier conditions - NOT for coding individual access factors or circumstances.

Name: Adaptive equipment (includes technology, like AAC devices and zoom/telehealth)
Notes: ONLY use when adaptive tech/equipment supports daily life: 'AAC device', 'adaptive equipment', 'assistive technology', 'telehealth for therapy', 'communication device' - NOT for normal technology use, standard medical equipment, or technology without adaptive purpose.

Name: Alternative Treatments Available
Notes: ONLY use when non-standard treatments are available/considered: 'alternative treatments', 'experimental therapy', 'non-traditional options', 'holistic approaches', 'complementary medicine' - NOT for standard treatments, conventional therapy, or mainstream medical options.

Name: Cleaner, Landscaper, Chef, Personal Assistant, Gardener, Dog Walker
Notes: any ancillary staff

Name: Educator (In Home)
Notes: Only use when an educator/teacher is specifically providing IN-HOME educational services. School-based educators, therapists visiting home for therapy, or tutors do NOT qualify.

Name: Early Intervention Program
Notes: ONLY use when EI services are in place for children under 3: 'early intervention', 'EI services', 'birth-to-three program', 'infant-toddler services', 'Part C services' - NOT for preschool services, school-age interventions, or services for children over 3.

Name: Healthcare Provider and Appointment Access
Notes: Use for experiences related to healthcare provider access — BOTH positive (reliable, knowledgeable, accessible provider, good specialist access) AND negative (difficulty finding providers, long wait times, lack of specialists, geographic barriers). In the rationale, specify whether the access experience is positive or negative. Do NOT use simply because a provider is mentioned — there must be a comment about the quality or accessibility of that access. NOTE: P8 team is considering splitting this into good-access and bad-access codes.

Name: In home medical care (nurses, therapies)
Notes: ONLY use when nurses/therapists provide care at home: 'home nursing', 'PT comes to home', 'in-home therapy', 'home health services', 'medical care at home' - NOT for clinic visits, outpatient therapy, or medical care outside the home.

Name: Information/Knowledge
Notes: ONLY use when knowledge resources/education influence care: 'learned about condition', 'education helped', 'information resources', 'knowledge about care', 'educational materials' - NOT for general learning, school education, or information without care influence.

Name: Diagnosis Identified
Notes: ONLY use when final diagnosis has been established: 'diagnosis confirmed', 'final diagnosis', 'diagnosed with', 'condition identified', 'diagnosis established' - NOT for suspected diagnoses, rule-outs, or diagnostic uncertainty without established diagnosis.

Name: Education of parent
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver education/training about condition is provided: 'parent education', 'trained about condition', 'taught caregiving skills', 'education program', 'caregiver training' - NOT for general education, school information, or child's education without caregiver training.

Name: Lack of information on disorder
Notes: ONLY use when caregivers report lack of information about condition: 'no information available', 'don't know about disorder', 'lack of resources', 'information gap', 'no guidance' - NOT for having information, partial information, or seeking additional information without expressing lack.

Name: Insurance
Notes: ONLY use when insurance coverage/limitations materially affect care: 'insurance denied', 'not covered', 'insurance limits care', 'coverage problems', 'insurance affects treatment' - NOT for routine coverage, insurance discussions, or coverage without material care impact.

Name: Legal assistance, child advocate
Notes: ONLY use when legal aid/advocacy services are used: 'child advocate', 'legal assistance', 'advocacy services', 'legal help', 'educational advocate' - NOT for normal school meetings, informal advocacy, or legal discussions without actual services.

Name: Regional differences in access to services, insurance and medical care
Notes: ONLY use when location affects access to services/insurance/care: 'rural area limits access', 'location affects services', 'regional differences', 'geographic barriers', 'area lacks services' - NOT for normal travel, convenience issues, or location mentions without access impact.

Name: Respite care
Notes: Child care

Name: Treatment for family members (anxiety medication, counseling)
Notes: ONLY use when family members receive treatment for condition-related issues: 'counseling for stress', 'anxiety medication for parent', 'therapy for caregiver', 'family member treatment' - NOT for unrelated medical care, general therapy, or treatment without condition connection.

Name: Family and Community Support Modifer
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Family and Community Support Modifier conditions - NOT for coding individual family or community support factors. Family history typically includes information from three generations of relatives, including children, siblings, parents, aunts and uncles, nieces and nephews, grandparents, and cousins. — NOT for coding individual family or community support factors or specific family history details.

Name: Community via facebook, foundation and support groups
Notes: ONLY use when online/support foundations provide community: 'Facebook support group', 'foundation community', 'online support', 'support group connections', 'community via groups' - NOT for general social media, unrelated groups, or community without support context.

Name: Supportive Community, including School
Notes: ONLY use when school/community support is strong: 'school very supportive', 'community helps', 'strong school support', 'supportive community', 'community rallies around' - NOT for basic services, neutral interactions, or minimal support without strong/significant support.

Name: Research, participation
Notes: ONLY use when family participates in research/registries: 'participating in research', 'enrolled in study', 'research participation', 'registry enrollment', 'clinical trial' - NOT for reading research, research discussions, or research without actual participation.

Name: Supportive family
Notes: ONLY use when extended family provides significant support: 'family very supportive', 'grandparents help a lot', 'strong family support', 'extended family assists', 'family rallies around' - NOT for minimal family help, normal family relationships, or family without significant support.

Name: Health Support Modifier
Notes: This is a CATEGORY HEADER code for organizing health support factors. Do NOT annotate this code directly for individual instances — instead use the specific child codes underneath it (e.g., [402] Multidisciplinary Care, [396] Supportive Community). Only annotate this code if the caregiver describes a health support factor that does not fit any specific child code.

Name: Bereavement counseling
Notes: ONLY use when grief counseling is used after losses: 'bereavement counseling', 'grief therapy', 'counseling after death', 'bereavement support', 'grief counselor' - NOT for general counseling, anticipatory grief, or support without actual bereavement counseling.

Name: Hospice
Notes: ONLY use when hospice services are engaged: 'hospice care', 'hospice services', 'end-of-life care', 'hospice team', 'hospice enrolled' - NOT for palliative care, comfort care, or end-of-life discussions without actual hospice services.

Name: Multidisciplinary Care
Notes: ONLY use when care is coordinated across specialties/teams: 'multidisciplinary team', 'coordinated care', 'team approach', 'multiple specialists work together', 'care coordination' - NOT for seeing multiple doctors, referrals, or specialists without team coordination.

Name: Palliative Care
Notes: ONLY use when palliative services are used: 'palliative care', 'comfort care team', 'palliative services', 'symptom management team', 'palliative specialist' - NOT for comfort measures, pain management, or supportive care without formal palliative services.

Name: Shared Decision Making
Notes: ONLY use when joint family-clinician decisions are emphasized: 'shared decision making', 'family involved in decisions', 'collaborative decisions', 'joint treatment planning', 'shared medical decisions' - NOT for normal consultations, information sharing, or decisions without explicit shared/collaborative approach.

Name: Pandemic (COVID)
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Pandemic-related modifying factors - NOT for coding individual pandemic impacts or symptoms.

Name: Resilience Modifier
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Resilience Modifier conditions - NOT for coding individual resilience factors or coping mechanisms.

Name: Coping strategies (caregivers)
Notes: ONLY use when caregivers describe coping methods that help: 'coping strategies', 'what helps me cope', 'coping mechanisms', 'strategies that work', 'helpful coping' - NOT for general activities, normal routines, or activities without explicit coping context.

Name: Spiritual beliefs
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [352] Work, reduced capacity. | Only use when caregiver describes spiritual/religious beliefs as a COPING mechanism or source of support. General mention of church or religion does NOT qualify.

Name: Severity of Symptoms (includes Seizure control)
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Severity of Symptoms conditions - NOT for coding individual symptom severity levels.

Name: Menses-related modification of symptoms
Notes: ONLY use when symptoms predictably change with menstrual cycle: 'symptoms worse during period', 'menses affect symptoms', 'menstrual cycle changes condition', 'symptoms fluctuate with periods' - NOT for general health variations, age-related changes, or symptom fluctuations without menstrual correlation.

Name: Work Modifier
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Work Modifier conditions - NOT for coding individual work-related factors or circumstances.

Name: Job flexiblity and financial resources
Notes: ONLY use when flexible employment or financial resources mitigate care impacts: 'flexible job helps', 'good insurance covers costs', 'financial resources ease burden', 'job flexibility allows care' - NOT for normal employment, typical benefits, or resources without specific impact mitigation.

Name: Professional Expertise of parent/caregivers
Notes: ONLY use when caregiver's professional background helps with patient care: 'I'm a nurse so I understand', 'my medical background helps', 'professional training aids caregiving', 'work experience helps with care' - NOT for general education, unrelated professional experience, or learning about condition without professional expertise benefit.

Name: MEDICAL INTERVENTIONS
Notes: REVIEW FEEDBACK: DO NOT confuse with [129] Fatigue.

Name: Adaptive or Special Equipment Required
Notes: ONLY use as a domain header for organizing Adaptive Equipment conditions - NOT for coding individual equipment needs or devices.

Name: Alternative and/or Augmentative Communication Devices Used
Notes: ONLY use when AAC devices are regularly used for communication: 'uses AAC device', 'has talker', 'iPad for speech', 'communication device', 'speech generating device' - NOT for occasional device use, apps without communication purpose, or devices not used for augmentative communication.

Name: Hearing Aids
Notes: ONLY use when patient uses hearing aids: 'wears hearing aids', 'hearing aids', 'hearing devices', 'amplification devices', or has documented hearing loss requiring the use of hearing aids — NOT for hearing tests, ear infections, hearing concerns without actual hearing aid use, or mentions of hearing loss that do not explicitly indicate the patient uses hearing aids

Name: Mobility Equipment
Notes: ONLY use when special mobility equipment is used: 'special chair', 'adaptive stroller', 'walker', 'mobility equipment', 'adaptive mobility devices' - NOT for normal strollers, regular chairs, or standard equipment without adaptive mobility purpose.

Name: AFOs '-' angle foot orhosis, orthotics
Notes: Only use when AFOs or orthotics are explicitly mentioned as currently used or prescribed. General mention of foot/ankle issues without orthotics does NOT qualify.

Name: Service Animal
Notes: ONLY use when trained service animal supports daily life: 'service dog', 'service animal', 'trained assistance animal', 'medical alert dog' - NOT for pets, emotional support animals, or therapy animals without formal service animal training.

Name: Standers, standing frame
Notes: ONLY use when standing equipment is used for positioning: 'stander', 'standing frame', 'standing device', 'positioning equipment for standing' - NOT for normal standing, standing practice, or mobility without standing equipment.

Name: Wheelchair
